title: Acinos × jahnianus
taxonomy: Acinos × jahnianus is a hybrid species, which is a cross between Acinos arvensis and Thymus lanuginosus. It belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This plant is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. It is typically found growing in dry, rocky areas and has a compact, shrubby growth habit. Acinos × jahnianus is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and small, blue-purple flowers.
uses: Acinos × jahnianus is a hybrid plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. It belongs to the mint family and has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Research on Acinos × jahnianus has found that it contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Acinos × jahnianus for medicinal purposes. Its uses in traditional medicine may include treating digestive issues and other health conditions, but more research is required to fully understand its effects.
benefits: Acinos × jahnianus is a plant species that has been studied for its potential health benefits. preparations: Acinos × jahnianus is often used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region. side_effects: Acinos × jahnianus information is limited, but as a member of the mint family, its side effects may be similar to those of other mint species. intro: Acinos × jahnianus, also known as Jahn's acinos or small mint, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, due to its calming and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Acinos × jahnianus include essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acinos × jahnianus is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and relieve symptoms of stress and insomnia.
